bonuses.leadin: Dorothy Scarborough’s dissertation provided a “suggestive rather than exhaustive” review of this genre “in Modern English Fiction.” For 10 points each:
parts: Virginia Woolf used Scarborough’s dissertation as the starting point for an essay titled for what genre “in Fiction,” which examines the “refined ... essence” of emotion experienced by readers?

Woolf’s essay traces the popularity of supernatural fiction to this author of the Gothic novel The Mysteries of Udolpho, claiming that her novels “may vanish, but the craving for the supernatural survives.”

Woolf praises the “superb genius” of this author’s ghost story “Wandering Willie’s Tale” from the novel Redgauntlet. This author recalled being “bewitched” by “strange” tales as a child in a passage from a book length poem about a battle that ends after Ralph De Wilton marries Clara de Clare.
answers: the supernatural [accept “The Supernatural in Modern English Fiction” or “The Supernatural in Fiction”; prompt on descriptive answers such as ghost stories or horror or scary fiction]
answers_sanitized: the supernatural [accept "The Supernatural in Modern English Fiction" or "The Supernatural in Fiction"; prompt on descriptive answers such as ghost stories or horror or scary fiction]
Walter Scott {The second poem is Marmion.}
